title: Lisa On Ice

jokes.text: Action News. The last place an impressionable kid can go for TV violence.
jokes.setup: Homer introducing Action News as if it's a good thing for kids
jokes.punchline: Revealing he values it specifically FOR the violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - praising something for exactly the reason others would criticize it. Great character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Kids, I want you to watch Action News. It's good for you.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: All that violence is great for teaching you about the real world.

jokes.text: A tremendous explosion in the price of lumber.
jokes.setup: Kent says 'tremendous explosion' suggesting actual violence
jokes.punchline: It's just lumber prices going up
jokes.explanation: Classic news language subversion - 'explosion' in prices vs actual explosion. Efficient wordplay.

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: We're reporting a tremendous explosion in the area.

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: It's just lumber prices going up.

jokes.text: bearing down on us like a shotgun full of snow
jokes.setup: Weather metaphor should be threatening
jokes.punchline: Shotgun full of snow is not actually dangerous
jokes.explanation: Absurd simile that sounds threatening but isn't. Nice Kent Brockman pompous language.

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: We're expecting a blizzard, folks. It'll hit like a shotgun full of snow.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: A shotgun full of snow? That doesn't sound dangerous at all.

jokes.text: As of now, the death count is zero. But it is ready to shoot right up.
jokes.setup: Death count should be reported soberly
jokes.punchline: He's almost excited about potential deaths
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about news media's obsession with tragedy. Kent's callousness is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: We interrupt this program to bring you a special news bulletin. A 9.0 earthquake has struck the Springfield area. We're still gathering information, but early reports indicate... possible deaths in the thousands.

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: More details as they become available. This is Kent Brockman.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Damn you, snow!
jokes.setup: Homer hearing about potential storm deaths
jokes.punchline: He dramatically curses the snow itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer overreaction - personalizing weather as an enemy. Great economy and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why you...

jokes.text: All right! Snow day! No school tomorrow!
jokes.setup: Homer cursing the snow
jokes.punchline: Bart celebrating the exact same snow
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast showing different perspectives on same event

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh! This blasted snow! Why does it have to ruin everything?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ay caramba! Snow day! School's cancelled! Cowabunga!

jokes.text: Lisa, that man is a professional meteorologist.
jokes.setup: Marge questions trusting the weatherman
jokes.punchline: Homer treats weatherman as infallible expert
jokes.explanation: Homer's misplaced faith in authority figures. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, why do you trust the weatherman so much? He's wrong half the time.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, the weatherman is never wrong. He's a scientist. They wear lab coats.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: But Homer, he said it would be sunny yesterday and it rained all day.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Exactly! He said it would be sunny, and it rained. Two predictions, one came true. That's a 50% success rate, which is better than my guess of 'weather happens.'

jokes.text: The forecast calls for a 75% chance of hilarity.
jokes.setup: Kent plugging his comedy show
jokes.punchline: Using weather forecast format for comedy performance
jokes.explanation: Clever format joke mixing meteorology and comedy. Efficient and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: Good evening. This is Kent Brockman with your weather forecast... I mean, comedy forecast. Tonight's high: hilarious. Low: you won't laugh. Chance of punchlines: 100 percent. Back to you.

jokes.text: I made the snowball from the frost in our freezer.
jokes.setup: Bart throws snowball despite warm weather
jokes.punchline: Lisa's practical solution reveals their poverty/resourcefulness
jokes.explanation: Great Lisa problem-solving logic. Shows family dynamics and economic status subtly.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I can't believe I'm throwing snowballs in this weather!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: That's not snow, Bart. That's the sherbet we've been saving for Dad's birthday.

jokes.text: Of course she did. Who else would have?
jokes.setup: Bully asks if mommy bought his pajamas
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact response deflates the insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant anti-joke. Bart's innocent logic completely disarms the intended insult. Perfect economy and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: Did your mommy buy you those pajamas?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.text: I'm saved! I love being a Simpson.
jokes.setup: Alphabetical order for book reports
jokes.punchline: Bart thinks being late in alphabet saves him
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart shortsighted thinking. Good setup for immediate reversal.

dialogue.speaker: Teacher
dialogue.line: Class, I want your book reports in alphabetical order by last name.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Aw man, Simpson starts with S. I'm gonna have to do mine early.

dialogue.speaker: Ralph
dialogue.line: My name starts with W!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... W comes after S. Ralph's got it made!

jokes.text: Let's see. We have no A's. So let's go right to the B's. Bart.
jokes.setup: Bart celebrating being saved by alphabetical order
jokes.punchline: Teacher immediately calls on him anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure - builds hope then immediately crushes it

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Bart Simpson!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Damn it, I wish we hadn't let the students name that.
jokes.setup: Announcement about 'Butthead Memorial Auditorium'
jokes.punchline: Skinner's immediate regret about the naming choice
jokes.explanation: Good institutional humor about unintended consequences of student democracy.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: I am pleased to announce that our auditorium will henceforth be known as the Butthead Memorial Auditorium.

dialogue.speaker: Principal Skinner
dialogue.line: I... may have made a terrible mistake.

jokes.text: Hey, Dolph, take a memo on your Newton: Beat up Martin.
jokes.setup: School introducing high-tech academic alerts
jokes.punchline: Bullies use technology for their own primitive purposes
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of technology adoption. Bullies adapting new tech for old purposes is very funny and era-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Jimbo
dialogue.line: Dude, check it out. We can use the school's new alert system to text the nerds we're gonna beat 'em up.

dialogue.speaker: Dolph
dialogue.line: Yeah, and they can't ignore it 'cause it's from the school.

dialogue.speaker: Kearney
dialogue.line: Technology rules!

jokes.text: I won! I won!
jokes.setup: Ralph receives academic alert for failing
jokes.punchline: He thinks it's an award
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph Wiggum moment - his innocent misunderstanding of negative feedback.

dialogue.speaker: Ralph
dialogue.line: An academic alert? I'm gonna be rich!

jokes.text: Me fail English? That's 'unpossible.'
jokes.setup: Ralph confused about failing English
jokes.punchline: Uses non-word 'unpossible' while denying English failure
jokes.explanation: Legendary Simpsons quote. Perfect irony - demonstrates English failure while denying it. Became cultural touchstone.

dialogue.speaker: Ralph
dialogue.line: I'm not failing English, I'm American!

dialogue.speaker: Ralph
dialogue.line: It's unpossible that I'm failing!

jokes.text: Hey! Keep it down, man.
jokes.setup: Nelson revealed to be good at home economics
jokes.punchline: Tough guy embarrassed about domestic skills
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - tough bully has hidden domestic talents.

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: Ha-ha! You got a problem with my home ec skills?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dude, you made a killer quilt in class today.

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: So? I can still break your arm.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: While wearing an apron you embroidered yourself?

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: Shut up!

jokes.text: Gym?! That's the stupidest thing I ever heard!
jokes.setup: Lisa discovers she's failing gym
jokes.punchline: Academic overachiever outraged at failing the 'easy' class
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa reaction - her intellectual pride wounded by failing physical education.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'm failing gym? But that's the easy class!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I have a 4.0 GPA in every subject that matters!

jokes.text: How about a present, son? / Well, I could use a new pair of hockey skates.
jokes.setup: Homer offers reward for not forging his name
jokes.punchline: Immediately rewards the wrong kid while Lisa gets punished
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamics humor showing Homer's favoritism and poor parenting logic.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'll give fifty bucks to whoever doesn't forge my name on a permission slip.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I didn't forge your name, Dad!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Here's fifty bucks, Ralph!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: But Dad, I'm the one who didn't forge your name.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Shut up, Lisa. You're grounded.

jokes.text: Ah, the mysteries of life.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks why Bart gets a present while she gets in trouble
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses obvious unfairness as mysterious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer deflection - treating his own bad parenting as philosophical mystery.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, why does Bart get a present when I get in trouble?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, Lisa, it's one of life's great mysteries, like why we park in driveways and drive on parkways.

jokes.text: You mean, where parents push kids into vicious competition to compensate for their own failed dreams of glory?
jokes.setup: Gym teacher suggests joining Pee Wee sports
jokes.punchline: Lisa's cynical but accurate assessment of youth sports
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary delivered through 8-year-old Lisa. Sharp satire of youth sports culture.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: You want me to join Pee Wee sports? Do you realize what that leads to? Steroids, scandals, and a lifetime of regret punctuated by occasional endorsement deals.

jokes.text: Look, I don't need this. I inhaled my favorite whistle this morning.
jokes.setup: Gym teacher getting frustrated with Lisa's attitude
jokes.punchline: Bizarre personal detail about whistle inhalation
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd detail that makes the gym teacher more pathetic and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Gym Teacher
dialogue.line: Listen here, Simpson! I'm tired of your attitude in my class!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: But Mr. Krupt, I'm just trying to maintain my physical and mental wellness through proper form.

dialogue.speaker: Gym Teacher
dialogue.line: Well, you know what? I've been coaching for thirty years, and I've inhaled so much whistle that I can now summon dogs just by breathing!

jokes.text: Children, that was our only ball. There'll be no team this year.
jokes.setup: Ball gets destroyed during practice
jokes.punchline: Entire season cancelled over one ball
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about institutional poverty and overreaction.

dialogue.speaker: Coach
dialogue.line: The entire season's cancelled over one ball?

jokes.text: Sports, sports, sports, sports
jokes.setup: Marge says sports are small part of life
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately contradicts with sports obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer contradiction and obsession. Good immediate reversal of Marge's point.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Sports are just a small part of life.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Small part?! Marge, sports are my entire life! Football, baseball, bowling, eating nachos while watching sports... That's basically four sports right there!

jokes.text: And if you lose, I'll kill you! / Oh, Dad.
jokes.setup: Homer tells Bart to have fun
jokes.punchline: Immediately threatens him with death for losing
jokes.explanation: Classic parental contradiction - supportive then threatening. Bart's casual response is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Have fun, Bart.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But if you lose, I'll kill you.

jokes.text: I could've been equipment manager, but no!
jokes.setup: Martin complaining while being forced to play hockey
jokes.punchline: His aspiration was the lowliest team position
jokes.explanation: Good Martin character moment - his modest ambitions and continued victimization.

dialogue.speaker: Martin
dialogue.line: I aspire to be the Zamboni driver!

jokes.text: Our goalie cannot stand up. / Tell you what, get some rope.
jokes.setup: Goalie is physically incapacitated
jokes.punchline: Solution is to tie him up rather than get new goalie
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about youth sports desperation and child endangerment.

dialogue.speaker: Coach
dialogue.line: Well, we can't get a new goalie, so I guess we'll just have to tie him up.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: Tie him up? But he can't even move!

dialogue.speaker: Coach
dialogue.line: Exactly. He won't be able to let any goals in if he's tied to the goal post.

jokes.text: Unfortunately, since I bet on the other team... we won't be going for pizza.
jokes.setup: Team celebrates victory
jokes.punchline: Homer bet against his own son's team
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Homer moment - terrible father betting against his own child. Perfect character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo! I won fifty bucks! Ah, sweet victory!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you bet against Bart's team?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, yeah. The boy's a loser. I'm not gonna lose fifty bucks because of him.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Thanks a lot, Dad.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, don't give me that look. You know I love you. But not fifty bucks worth.

jokes.text: Wow, Bart, I'm so impressed you were able to give Milhouse a concussion.
jokes.setup: Bart wants praise from Lisa
jokes.punchline: She sarcastically praises him for injuring his friend
jokes.explanation: Great Lisa sarcasm highlighting the violence of hockey through her moral lens.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Lisa, aren't you gonna say something nice about me?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Oh Bart, I'm so proud of you for breaking Milhouse's arm. What an accomplishment. You've really outdone yourself this time.

jokes.text: What the...? / You know, I wonder if her skills will transfer over to the game of hockey.
jokes.setup: Lisa makes an impressive save against Bart
jokes.punchline: Apu immediately spots her hockey potential
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Lisa's hockey arc. Bart's shocked reaction to being shown up.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Lisa, you have tremendous hockey talent! You must join my team at the Kwik-E-Mart Hockey League!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Whoa, Apu, she's MY sister. Back off!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: But Bart, the Kwik-E-Mart needs her. Think of the slurpee sales!

jokes.text: The goalie of my dreams!
jokes.setup: Apu testing Lisa's goalie skills
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top enthusiasm for finding a goalie
jokes.explanation: Decent Apu enthusiasm moment, though not particularly original.

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Lisa, you must be our new goalie! Finally, someone to stop the opposing team from scoring!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: Do you know what this means? Do you?! We can finally win a game! Maybe even two games!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: I have been searching for a goalie for so long, I was beginning to think they were mythical creatures, like the yeti or a man who doesn't love donuts!

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: You are a gift from the soccer gods themselves!

jokes.text: Lisa, if the Bible has taught us nothing else, and it hasn't... it's that girls should stick to girl sports.
jokes.setup: Homer making biblical argument about gender roles
jokes.punchline: Casually admits the Bible taught him nothing
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - undermines his own argument mid-sentence. Perfect character voice and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know, the Bible says that a woman should not speak in church. It also says that if a woman speaks in church, she should cover her head. But most importantly, it says that I should be the head of the household.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's not what the Bible says.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, it should. Anyway, the point is, I've learned absolutely nothing from the Bible.

jokes.text: Such as hot oil wrestling, foxy boxing and such and such.
jokes.setup: Homer listing appropriate 'girl sports'
jokes.punchline: Lists sexualized entertainment as sports
jokes.explanation: Good Homer crudeness and sexism revealing his true motivations.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, there's ice skating, gymnastics, and synchronized swimming.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, and beach volleyball.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Ooh, and mud wrestling!

jokes.text: Stop the inauguration! I just discovered our president-elect got an F in second-grade gym class!
jokes.setup: Lisa imagining her future ruined by gym grade
jokes.punchline: Fantasy shows F in gym preventing presidency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa anxiety and perfectionism. Absurd escalation of academic consequences.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'm doomed! A single F in gym will keep me out of the Ivy League! Without an elite education, I'll never get into politics. And without politics, I'll never become president!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: My dreams of the White House are finished before they even started.

jokes.text: Don't worry. It's just a name. / He said it was just a name! / What he meant is that Monster Island is actually a peninsula.
jokes.setup: Judge reassures Lisa about Monster Island
jokes.punchline: Clarification makes it worse - it's real but technically a peninsula
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity - false comfort followed by worse but pedantically accurate truth.

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: Don't worry, Lisa. It's not a real monster island.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Oh, thank God.

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: It's actually a peninsula.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: Monster Peninsula.

jokes.text: Look! That kid's got bosoms! Who's got a wet towel?
jokes.setup: Homer warns against giving Lisa hard time
jokes.punchline: Kid immediately targets her for having female anatomy
jokes.explanation: Crude but realistic kid humor about gender differences in sports.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Listen up, all of you. I don't want any of you giving Lisa a hard time.

dialogue.speaker: Nelson
dialogue.line: Ha-ha! Flat chest!

jokes.text: Don't make me run! I'm full of chocolate!
jokes.setup: Martin being chased by bullies
jokes.punchline: His excuse for not running is chocolate consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin excuse - nerdy, pathetic, and oddly specific. Good character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Martin
dialogue.line: I would flee, but I've consumed a large quantity of chocolate, which has rendered my legs immobile.

jokes.text: We're laughing with her, Marge. There's a big difference. / With her.
jokes.setup: Marge criticizes them for laughing at Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer's hollow correction followed by uncertain repetition
jokes.explanation: Great Homer deflection and self-doubt. The repeated 'with her' shows his uncertainty.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We're not laughing at Lisa.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We're laughing... near Lisa.

jokes.text: Sucker! Competitive violence, that's why you're here!
jokes.setup: Lisa thinks Homer's gesture is fatherly love
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately reveals his true violent motivations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - can't maintain his facade for even seconds. Great character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, that's so sweet. You really do care about me.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Of course I do, sweetie. Now let me show you how much... by strangling you!

jokes.text: Gee, who am I kidding, huh? It's all because of Lisa.
jokes.setup: Coach tries to credit teamwork for success
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits it's just one player
jokes.explanation: Good honest coach moment abandoning sports clichés for truth.

dialogue.speaker: Coach
dialogue.line: Remember team, there's no 'I' in team. That's why we won today — through teamwork and cooperation.

dialogue.speaker: Coach
dialogue.line: Well, okay, it was mostly Bart. But the rest of you... you didn't actively make us lose!

jokes.text: Hey, Bart. If Lisa's better than you at hockey... does that mean you're gonna become better than her at school?
jokes.setup: Siblings competing in different areas
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's innocent logic about role reversal
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse innocent observation setting up Bart's academic efforts.

dialogue.speaker: Milhouse
dialogue.line: If my sister's better at sports and I'm better at academics, doesn't that make me the jock and her the nerd?

jokes.text: Who can tell me the capital of Spain? Bart Simpson. The square root of 36? Bart Simpson. Who freed the slaves? Bart Simpson.
jokes.setup: Bart enthusiastically raising his hand in class
jokes.punchline: Teacher calling on him for every question regardless of topic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and teacher frustration. Shows Bart's newfound but misguided enthusiasm.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ooh! Ooh! Ooh!

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Yes, Bart?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: What's the capital of France?

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: That's correct, Bart. Very good.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Now, can anyone tell me the chemical formula for water?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ooh! Ooh! Ooh!

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Yes, Bart?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: H2O!

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Correct again, Bart.

jokes.text: Bart Simpson, stop raising your hand. You haven't had one right answer today.
jokes.setup: Bart's academic enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Teacher's blunt assessment of his complete failure
jokes.explanation: Good teacher exasperation and reality check for Bart's efforts.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Bart, your essay on the American Revolution was due last week.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I know, Mrs. Krabappel. I was really busy.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: Well, I finally read it. Bart, this is the worst thing I've ever seen. You didn't even spell 'Revolution' correctly. And you wrote the whole thing in crayon.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: So... what grade did I get?

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Krabappel
dialogue.line: An F. Actually, I invented a new grade just for you: a Z-minus. Congratulations, you've achieved academic mediocrity that shouldn't be possible.

jokes.text: It's a lucky coincidence you happen to be your sister's brother.
jokes.setup: Lisa protecting Bart from bullies
jokes.punchline: Bully's redundant way of stating family relationship
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from bully's unnecessarily complex statement of obvious fact.

dialogue.speaker: Jimbo
dialogue.line: Hey, that's our job to bully Bart, not yours!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Back off, he's my brother.

dialogue.speaker: Jimbo
dialogue.line: Well, he's OUR brother too... I mean, not literally, but we've bullied him since he was our brother's age.

jokes.text: I hardly ever let Mother fight for me anymore.
jokes.setup: Martin grateful for Lisa's protection
jokes.punchline: Implies his mother usually fights his battles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin pathetic moment - proud of minimal independence from maternal protection.

dialogue.speaker: Martin
dialogue.line: Thank you, Lisa. You've saved me once again. Usually it's my mother who comes to my rescue with her formidable legal expertise and threatening letters.

jokes.text: Don't have a stereotypical view of me just because I'm your mother. / I know. How about we play the basketball?
jokes.setup: Marge warns against stereotyping mothers
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggests stereotypical awkward sports activity
jokes.explanation: Great Marge moment - self-aware but still falls into the exact stereotype. 'Play the basketball' is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: We shouldn't stereotype mothers. Not all of us are the same.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Now if you'll excuse me, I have a very important aerobics class to attend in my pastel tracksuit.

jokes.text: I'm no Harvey Globetrotter, but...
jokes.setup: Marge attempting sports metaphor
jokes.punchline: Mixes up Harlem Globetrotters with Harvey something
jokes.explanation: Good Marge malapropism showing her unfamiliarity with sports culture.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, we need to work together as a team, like the Harlem Globetrotters!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, don't you mean Harvey Wallbangers?

jokes.text: Watch out for the Shaq attack!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to be cool with basketball slang
jokes.punchline: Using dated/awkward sports terminology
jokes.explanation: Good Marge trying too hard to relate to sports culture with dated references.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: That's totally tubular, dude! Those cats are really dunking the rock down the court!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I'm being hip to the jive! Totally rad and groovy with the basketball!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Mom, nobody talks like that anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, the referee just gave them a technical foul for excessive jam-itude!

jokes.text: Well, not wasted... I love you.
jokes.setup: Homer says time with Bart was wasted
jokes.punchline: Awkward backtrack when he realizes how cruel it sounds
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment of realizing his cruelty and attempting damage control.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: All those years with Bart were a complete waste of time.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I mean, uh... not a waste. Quality time. The best years of my life.

jokes.text: Ma. I think I'm all hugged out. / Ah, one more.
jokes.setup: Lisa getting overwhelmed by attention
jokes.punchline: Marge ignoring her boundaries for more affection
jokes.explanation: Decent family dynamic humor about parental over-enthusiasm.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Mom, please, I need some space.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh sweetie, you know Mommy needs her hugs!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: But Mom, you're suffocating me—

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Nonsense! One more squeeze!

jokes.text: But instead, I just ripped the head off Mr. Honey Bunny!
jokes.setup: Bart talks about resolving differences civilly
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals violent destruction of childhood toy
jokes.explanation: Great Bart escalation from civility to violence. The toy name 'Mr. Honey Bunny' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: You know, I've learned that the best way to resolve differences is through calm, rational discussion.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: That's why I sat down with my old teddy bear and we talked things through... then I beat it to death with a baseball bat.

jokes.text: Bart, that was your cherished childhood toy.
jokes.setup: Bart destroys Mr. Honey Bunny to hurt Lisa
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out he hurt himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa logical response highlighting Bart's self-destructive revenge.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: But Bart, Mr. Honey Bunny was your toy too.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: So?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: So you just destroyed a part of your own childhood.

jokes.text: If you get hit, it's your own fault.
jokes.setup: Bart doing exaggerated windmill punches
jokes.punchline: Classic playground disclaimer for random violence
jokes.explanation: Classic kid logic for avoiding responsibility while being deliberately provocative.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ay caramba! I'm doing the windmill!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Everybody back! I'm not responsible for any injuries!

jokes.text: All right, pie, I'm just gonna do this: And if you get eaten, it's your own fault.
jokes.setup: Homer told not to eat pie
jokes.punchline: Uses Bart's exact logic to justify eating it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback showing Homer adopting his son's childish logic. Perfect structure and character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But Marge said I couldn't eat pie... Bart, what would you do?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Well, Dad, if someone told me not to do something, I'd just do it anyway and say 'Eat my shorts!'

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Eat my shorts! That's it! Marge never said I couldn't eat pie if I said 'Eat my shorts!' first.

jokes.text: Hey! Apu just called. This Friday, Lisa's team is playing Bart's team.
jokes.setup: Homer telling kids they're not in competition
jokes.punchline: Immediately announces direct competition
jokes.explanation: Good immediate contradiction setup. Homer's timing is perfect for undermining Marge's message.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Kids, I want you to know that you're not in competition with each other.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Now, whoever gets the best grades this semester wins a new mountain bike!

jokes.text: Don't go easy on each other because you're brother and sister. I want to see you both fighting for your parents' love!
jokes.setup: Typical sports parent encouragement
jokes.punchline: Homer explicitly wants them competing for love
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark parenting moment. Homer saying the quiet part loud about conditional love and sibling rivalry.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Come on, boy! You gotta want it more than Bart!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, we're on the same team.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I don't care! I want you two fighting for my love out there!

jokes.text: All right. All right, I'll sweeten the deal. You can see the game, you don't have to come back... but you have to promise not to commit any more crimes, okay? / No.
jokes.setup: Wiggum negotiating with prisoners
jokes.punchline: Prisoners refuse deal specifically about not committing crimes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence and criminal honesty. Great absurdist law enforcement.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Alright, fellas, here's the deal. You stop committing crimes, and we'll cut your sentences in half.

dialogue.speaker: Prisoner 1
dialogue.line: Not committing crimes? That's asking too much!

dialogue.speaker: Prisoner 2
dialogue.line: Yeah, we're prisoners! That's what we do!

jokes.text: Their father's fat and their mother's thin And Grampa Simpson reeks of gin / Hey! That's Obsession for Men.
jokes.setup: Crowd chanting insults about Simpson family
jokes.punchline: Grampa corrects them about his cologne choice
jokes.explanation: Good Grampa vanity moment. His priorities are perfectly wrong - cares about cologne brand, not insult.

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: Simpson! Simpson! Simpson!

dialogue.speaker: Grampa
dialogue.line: It's not Brut, it's Hai Karate!

jokes.text: I hope you understand I'm too tense to pretend I like you.
jokes.setup: Moe making friendly small talk
jokes.punchline: Marge's brutally honest response
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Marge moment. Polite but devastatingly honest. Perfect economy and quotability.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: So Marge, how's everything going? You look great today!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Moe, that's sweet of you to say, but we both know I haven't slept in three days, my hair is held together with bobby pins and desperation, and I'm pretty sure this is the same outfit I wore last Tuesday.

jokes.text: But Blanche, you gotta help me out here! Please! I'm 64 grand in the hole! They're gonna take my thumbs!
jokes.setup: Moe asking for gambling information
jokes.punchline: Reveals massive debt and specific threatened violence
jokes.explanation: Good Moe desperation moment. Specific threat of thumb removal is appropriately absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Hey, I need some gambling information. What's the over-under on the Broncos game?

dialogue.speaker: Bookie
dialogue.line: That'll be $500, Moe.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: I don't have $500. I'm already in for $43,000.

dialogue.speaker: Bookie
dialogue.line: You got until Tuesday to pay up, or we break your legs.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Both of 'em?

jokes.text: I'm wearing my lucky rabbit's head.
jokes.setup: Good luck wishes before game
jokes.punchline: Gruesome lucky charm instead of rabbit's foot
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on luck superstitions, sets up Mr. Honey Bunny reveal

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Here's a lucky charm for the game.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: A real rabbit's foot.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Cool! Thanks, Lis.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Oh, and the rest of the rabbit is in the freezer if you need it.

jokes.text: Mr. Honey Bunny! You inhumane monster!
jokes.setup: Lisa sees Bart wearing destroyed toy as lucky charm
jokes.punchline: Her horror at the toy's desecration
jokes.explanation: Good callback and Lisa's moral outrage. Calling Bart 'inhumane monster' is strong.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart! What have you done to Mr. Fuzzyboots?!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: It's my lucky charm now. See? One eye's hanging off, the stuffing's coming out...

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: That's disgusting! You've desecrated a perfectly good toy!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Perfectly good? Lisa, it's been lucky for three whole days.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: The only thing that's lucky is that I'm not the one who has to look at that abomination.

jokes.text: You can't possible like Maggie best. What has she ever done? Nothing for nobody.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Marge to pick favorite child
jokes.punchline: Dismisses baby for lack of accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic applying adult productivity standards to a baby. Dark but funny.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, you gotta pick your favorite child.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, I can't do that. I love all our children equally.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I can. Bart's my favorite.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: What about Lisa and Maggie?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Lisa's smart, so that's something. But Maggie? What's she done? She can't even talk. She just drools and cries. Until she accomplishes something, she's out.

jokes.text: Shouldn't have turned down those cue cards.
jokes.setup: Krusty presumably butchering national anthem
jokes.punchline: His regret about professional help
jokes.explanation: Good Krusty incompetence moment. Implies disaster we didn't see.

dialogue.speaker: Krusty
dialogue.line: I should've gotten professional help for my singing.

jokes.text: I love Bart! / No, wait! I love Lisa! / Beer? Oh, Marge, I love you.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to choose which kid to support
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by beer and chooses Marge instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - torn between kids until beer offers better option. Great character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Okay, I gotta choose which kid to support. Bart or Lisa?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... beer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I choose Marge!

jokes.text: It's your child versus mine! The winner will be showered with praise! The loser will be taunted and booed until my throat is sore!
jokes.setup: Penalty shot creates drama
jokes.punchline: Homer's commitment to extended booing
jokes.explanation: Great Homer sports parent escalation. The throat soreness detail makes his commitment funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Boo! Boo! Boo! Boo! Boo! Boo! Boo! Boo! Boo! Boo!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, the game's over.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I don't care! I committed to this boo!

jokes.text: Tie game?! / What the hell?! / This is outrageous.
jokes.setup: Siblings choose not to compete against each other
jokes.punchline: Crowd outraged at sportsmanship
jokes.explanation: Good satire of sports fan bloodlust. They want competition over family bonds.

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: Boo! Boo! You stink!

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: Yeah, show some school spirit! Destroy each other!

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: What kind of sportsmanship is this?!

jokes.text: They're both losers. Losers! / Rip off! / We paid for blood!
jokes.setup: Crowd disappointed in tie game
jokes.punchline: Explicit demand for violence from children's sports
jokes.explanation: Dark but accurate satire of sports entertainment culture demanding violence.

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: Boo! Boo! We want a winner!

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: Kill the ref! Kill the ref!

jokes.text: Those kids are, like, so sweet.
jokes.setup: Crowd rioting over disappointing game
jokes.punchline: Teen's oblivious comment amid chaos
jokes.explanation: Mild contrast humor - sincere sentiment during chaos. Not particularly strong.

dialogue.speaker: Teen
dialogue.line: This is awesome!

jokes.text: If only they had had Pee Wee hockey when I was a lad.
jokes.setup: Wiggum watching chaos unfold
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic for childhood opportunity to cause similar chaos
jokes.explanation: Decent Wiggum moment missing the point and romanticizing violence.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Boy, I wish I was a kid again. I could cause all this chaos and nobody would care.

tmdb_overview: Lisa's surprising ability at hockey spawns jealousy in Bart and a showdown when their teams meet.
insight: Hockey rivalry escalates beautifully: 70 jokes across 22 minutes hit character gold.
